description: Egress-renormalization invariant (citation-numbering family, landed with the fix per the R12 rule): two raw inline markers resolving to the SAME deduped chunk share one displayed number. Provenance: plan 2026-06-11-001 (mapping is marker -> chunk_id -> number, never positional). Construction: document_chunks loads 7 chunks; the pre-fetch (P-201 resolution) registers chunks 1-5 as visible [1]..[5], and the scripted search_documents call serves the next window of 2, registered as [6]..[7] -- where slot 7 carries the SAME chunk_id as slot 2 (a re-retrieved chunk: _register_document_results appends the duplicate at both ordered positions while the registry dict keeps one entry). The model cites [2] and [7] inline and in the block; parse_response dedups by chunk_id to ONE citation, and the renormalizer maps both raw markers through that chunk_id to the same number 1. The footer has exactly one entry.
user_messages: What is the vibration alarm threshold for P-201?

document_chunks.content: P-201 overview: cooling water pump, criticality A.
Vibration alarm threshold: 7.1 mm/s RMS per ISO 10816-3 zone C/D boundary.
Seal replacement procedure: depressurize, drain, pull cartridge.
Check coupling alignment annually or after any seal work.
Grease the bearing housings with NLGI 2 grease every 500 hours.
Replace the drive-end bearing every 8000 operating hours.
Vibration alarm threshold: 7.1 mm/s RMS per ISO 10816-3 zone C/D boundary.

turns.content:
The vibration alarm threshold is 7.1 mm/s RMS [2]. Investigate the bearings whenever that threshold is exceeded [7].

<citations>
[2]
[7]
</citations>
